title: Five Eyes warn on AI cyberattacks as defense diffuses - TCR 06/23/26
description:
Five Eyes intelligence agencies warned that frontier-AI cyberattack capability is months away as OpenAI launched a free Patch the Planet coalition arming open-source defenders, AI super PACs poured $49 million into a single Manhattan House primary targeting the RAISE Act sponsor, and an AI law firm won a contested English court trial preparing a full case for about £400.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-23-2026/

0:00 Five Eyes warning and the defensive answer
0:43 AI super PACs target a state safety law
3:55 An AI law firm wins an English trial
7:00 The £400 Courtroom
9:41 Facial recognition enters everyday public space
12:35 The agentic loop: agents prompting agents
15:32 Synthesis and Closing

This episode covers the Five Eyes cyber warning and OpenAI's Patch the Planet defense coalition, the $49 million super PAC blitz against New York's AI safety-disclosure law, Garfield AI's first English trial win, live facial recognition fixing to London street furniture and Kansas City buses, and the agentic "loops" where AI agents prompt other agents to write code.

A single fault line runs under the day: who gets to hold a capability that keeps refusing to stay held. Five nations' signals agencies urged leaders to act on machine-speed cyberattacks days after the administration walled Anthropic's Fable off from foreign nationals, while OpenAI and the earlier Athena coalition handed individualized vulnerability-finding to the volunteer maintainers holding up critical software. The same diffusion of expertise reached a Wandsworth courtroom, where Garfield AI built a full case for £400 and won, narrowing the knowledge gap professions have rested on. Four donors concentrated $49 million on one primary to punish a state safety law, even as states keep writing AI rules faster than any one race can erase them. London bolts facial-recognition cameras to lamp-posts and Kansas City eyes its buses, forcing consent-and-accountability frameworks into law ahead of saturation, and the agentic frontier goes "loopy" as agents prompt agents to improve code overnight while a human sets what "better" means.

Additional Reading:
- The Guardian - Five Eyes Warn AI Cyber Capability Is "Months, Not Years" Away: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/22/anthropic-claude-fable-ai-model-artificial-intelligence-national-security
- Wired - OpenAI Launches Full-Scale Effort to Patch Open-Source Bugs: https://www.wired.com/story/openai-launches-full-scale-effort-to-patch-open-source-bugs-as-it-takes-on-anthropics-mythos/
- The Guardian - AI Super PACs Target a New York House Primary: https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/22/new-york-city-house-primary-race
- The Guardian - AI Law Firm Wins a Court Case in England for the First Time: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/22/artificial-intelligence-law-firm-wins-court-case-in-england-for-first-time
